monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Maranao art, epic, and cultural tradition
monsters.passive_ability: Omen of Radiance — At the start of battle, raises party Luck and Faith. During Clear, Dawn, or Spirit Glow weather, reveals Hidden enemies.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Okir Feather Benediction — Scatters luminous feathers that heal allies, increase Luck, and cleanse Fear or Despair.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Crown of the Sun-Rooster — Releases a radiant sky cry that damages all enemies with Divine/Sky energy and grants Hopeful to allies.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Sacred Light, Wind Slash, Haste, Blessing, Reveal, Hopeful Hymn, Cleanse, Spirit Ward, Feather Guard, Sunbeam, Calm, Barrier, Omen Call, Counter Ready, Regeneration
monsters.capture_difficulty: Legendary Trial / Trust Difficulty: Requires completing the Lake Omen Festival and restoring a lost okir relic
monsters.habitat: Highland Village, Mountain Peak, Spirit Threshold, Sacred Shrine, Moonlit Clearing
monsters.active_time: Dawn, Day, Festival Only, Story Triggered
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Golden rice, okir-carved charm, woven cloth, flower garland, dawn water
monsters.lore_and_backstory: The Sarimanok is a radiant bird of omen and prestige, deeply associated with Maranao cultural art and symbolism. In ALAMAT, the Sarimanok is not merely a beautiful creature; it is a messenger of balance whose feathers carry colors of dawn, lake, brass, and song. It appears only when a community's memory of artistry and honor is threatened. The player encounters Sarimanok during a festival where the ceremonial okir relic has been stolen, causing the lake winds to fall silent and the morning birds to refuse to sing. To earn its trust, the player must recover the relic, help artisans complete the festival patterns, and choose humility over fame. Once bonded, Sarimanok becomes a legendary oracle-support Nilalang that heals allies, reveals hidden threats, manipulates luck, and turns beauty into battle power. Its role in ALAMAT is to remind players that culture itself can be a sacred force worth defending.
monsters.folklore_summary: Sarimanok is a legendary bird motif strongly associated with Maranao culture, often depicted as a colorful bird with ornate okir designs and sometimes holding a fish. It is a major symbol in art, identity, and cultural storytelling.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT treats Sarimanok as a divine omen bird and cultural guardian whose power comes from artistry, dawn, honor, and remembered tradition.
monsters.physical_description: A magnificent rooster-like celestial bird with jewel-toned feathers, flowing okir-inspired tail plumes, golden crown crest, brass anklets, sunlit eyes, and a glowing fish-shaped spirit ornament carried beneath its beak.
monsters.weaknesses: Corruption, Silence, broken festival rites, shadow eclipses, dishonored vows
monsters.resistances: Divine, Sky, Sun, Fear, Despair, Blind, Charm
monsters.npc_related: Apo Tala the sky watcher; Bai Amirah the okir artisan; Datu Salin the festival judge
monsters.boss_variant: Sarimanok ng Pitong Kulay — shifts feather colors to change buffs each phase.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Sarimanok ng Kupas na Araw — faded omen bird that turns blessings into confusion.
monsters.divine_variant: Sarimanok, Bantay ng Bukang-Liwayway — radiant dawn guardian with golden okir wings.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A proud rooster-like crow layered with brass gongs, lake wind, and bright morning birdsong.
monsters.drop_items: Okir Feather, Dawn Brass Bead, Radiant Plume, Omen Fish Charm
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Beadwork, Headdress, Instrument, Anting-Anting, Divine Talisman
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG legendary sprite of Sarimanok inspired by Maranao art: radiant colorful omen bird, ornate okir-inspired feathers, golden crown crest, flowing tail plumes, brass accents, glowing fish charm, dawn aura, transparent background, readable chibi RPG silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Epic fantasy portrait of Sarimanok, radiant Maranao-inspired omen bird, jewel-toned feathers, okir patterns, golden crest, brass ornaments, dawn lake background, majestic and culturally respectful RPG art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Sarimanok spreads okir-patterned wings, feathers become glowing glyphs, a dawn sun rises behind it, allies receive golden light, and enemies are struck by a ringing sky cry.
monsters.design_notes: Use respectfully as Maranao-inspired cultural guardian. Avoid generic phoenix treatment; emphasize okir, dawn, omen, and lake-culture identity.

monsters.province_origin: Mandaya and Manobo cultural regions of eastern/southern Mindanao
monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Mandaya, Manobo, and neighboring Mindanao omen-bird traditions
monsters.passive_ability: Left-and-Right Omen — The first time an ally would miss each battle, Limokon grants a small accuracy correction. If an enemy is Marked, allies gain bonus Luck against it.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Call from the Right Branch — A favorable omen cry increases ally accuracy, Luck, and critical chance for two turns.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Warning from the Left Branch — A sharp omen cry reduces enemy accuracy and may apply Marked or Fear.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Wind Dash, Reveal, Omen Call, Haste, Feather Guard, Spirit Ward, Blessing, Mark Prey, Calm, Cleanse, Quick Peck, Evasive Flight, Hopeful Hymn, Barrier, Ancestral Guard
monsters.capture_difficulty: Medium-Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires following omen calls correctly during a forest journey
monsters.habitat: Balete Grove, Bamboo Thicket, Highland Village, Mountain Peak, Rice Terraces
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Millet grains, forest berries, woven perch ring, clean water, ancestor prayer
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Limokon is remembered in Mindanao traditions as an omen bird whose calls could guide decisions, journeys, and interpretations of danger or fortune. In ALAMAT, Limokon are not fortune-tellers in a shallow sense; they are listening birds that hear the emotional direction of the world. This Limokon appears when travelers repeatedly disappear in a forest because they ignore every warning sign: broken twigs, reversed wind, and the bird's call from the left. To earn its trust, the player must complete a forest route by obeying omen calls rather than map markers. Once bonded, Limokon becomes a fast support Nilalang that improves accuracy, reveals danger, marks threats, and protects the party from bad luck. It is small compared with legendary birds, but its guidance can decide whether an expedition succeeds or fails.
monsters.folklore_summary: Limokon is an omen bird known in several Mindanao traditions, including Mandaya accounts, where its call and direction could be interpreted as signs of good or bad fortune.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT turns Limokon into a guide-spirit whose cries read the moral direction of journeys and warn against arrogance.
monsters.physical_description: A small elegant dove-like bird with soft gray-blue feathers, white chest, gold-ringed eyes, faint ancestor glyphs on its wings, and a subtle breeze trail following its flight.
monsters.weaknesses: Storm, Silence, trickster noise, poisoned grain, corrupted omen stones
monsters.resistances: Sky, Ancestor, Spirit, Blind, Fear, confusion from natural terrain
monsters.npc_related: Bae Luntaya the herbalist; Ramo the trail keeper; Apo Tala the omen reader
monsters.boss_variant: Limokon ng Apat na Sanga — creates four omen branches with different battlefield effects.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Maling-Omen Limokon — reverses good and bad signs, causing allies to misread danger.
monsters.divine_variant: Limokon ng Tamang Daan — ancestor-guided dove with golden wing glyphs.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A gentle dove call that sounds different depending on direction, followed by a faint ancestral chime.
monsters.drop_items: Omen Feather, Ancestor Grain, Wing Glyph Dust, Forest Perch Twig
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Beadwork, Bell, Instrument, Anting-Anting, Feather Charm
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Limokon inspired by Mindanao omen bird traditions: small elegant dove-like bird, gray-blue feathers, white chest, gold-ringed eyes, subtle ancestor glyphs on wings, soft wind trail, transparent background, readable chibi RPG support creature style.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Limokon, Philippine omen dove, soft gray-blue feathers, gold-ringed eyes, wing glyphs, forest branch at dawn, gentle but mystical RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Limokon flies from the right branch to the left branch, its cry creates golden directional glyphs, allies gain accuracy while enemies are marked by a warning feather.
monsters.design_notes: Small but strategically important. Use omen mechanics for accuracy, luck, and path choices.

monsters.province_origin: Bukidnon, Visayas, and wider Philippine ghoul folklore
monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Multiple regional traditions; often grouped with aswang-like corpse eaters
monsters.passive_ability: Grave-Scent Tracker — Deals bonus damage to enemies affected by Poison, Rot, Doom, or Despair. Gains evasion in Graveyard habitats.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Corpse-Scent Pounce — Tracks the weakest enemy by spiritual scent, dealing Underworld damage and applying Marked.
monsters.unique_skill_2: False Funeral Mist — Creates an illusionary burial fog that hides Bal-Bal and applies Poison or Blind to enemies.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Shadow Step, Bite, Poison Fang, Mark Prey, Hidden, Fear Cry, Rot, Dark Pulse, Quick Claw, Weaken, Ambush, Smoke Veil, Curse Bite, Evasive Step, Spirit Leak
monsters.capture_difficulty: Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires protecting a burial from grave thieves and cleansing false-body magic
monsters.habitat: Graveyard, Corrupted Wilds, Bamboo Thicket, Ancestral House, Spirit Threshold
monsters.weather_preference: Fog, Moonlit, New Moon, Spirit Glow
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Burial flowers, black salt, incense ash, grave clay, name ribbon
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Bal-Bal is feared as a corpse-stealing ghoul, sometimes said to replace the dead with banana trunks or false bodies after stealing from graves. In ALAMAT, Bal-Bal are drawn to places where death has been commodified, hidden, or disrespected. This Bal-Bal prowls a cemetery after a group of thieves begins selling burial ornaments and replacing the dead with enchanted decoys. Villagers blame the ghoul for every disappearance, but the player discovers that the Bal-Bal is both guilty and manipulated: it was lured by corruption released from the stolen ornaments. To earn trust, the player must expose the thieves, return the ornaments, and cleanse the false bodies. Once bonded, Bal-Bal becomes a stealth assassin that tracks weakened targets through scent, hides in grave mist, and punishes enemies already touched by decay.
monsters.folklore_summary: Bal-Bal is a Philippine folklore ghoul or aswang-like being associated with stealing and eating corpses, sometimes replacing the body with a banana trunk or deceptive substitute.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT presents Bal-Bal as a grave-scent predator connected to burial disrespect, theft, and false appearances surrounding death.
monsters.physical_description: A thin, long-limbed ghoul with gray-purple skin, glowing nostrils, sharp teeth, hunched back, ragged funeral cloth, long digging claws, and mist curling from its mouth like cold grave breath.
monsters.weaknesses: Sacred burial rites, Sun, bell wards, incense cleansing, truthful corpse-name rituals
monsters.resistances: Underworld, Poison, Shadow, Rot, Fear, grave mist concealment
monsters.npc_related: Apo Samad the grave keeper; Lito the ornament thief; Mira the mourning child
monsters.boss_variant: Bal-Bal ng Huwad na Libing — creates false corpse decoys that explode into poison mist.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Bulok-Bal-Bal — fully rotten ghoul that spreads Rot with every movement.
monsters.divine_variant: Bantay ng Tunay na Libing — cleansed grave guardian that exposes false bodies.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A wet sniffing hiss followed by a scraping claw rhythm under the soil.
monsters.drop_items: Grave Mist Cloth, False Body Fiber, Bal-Bal Claw, Black Salt Ash
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Bone Talisman, Spirit Mask, Bell, Anting-Anting, Tattoo Mark
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Bal-Bal inspired by Philippine folklore: thin corpse-stealing ghoul, gray-purple skin, long digging claws, ragged funeral cloth, glowing nostrils, grave mist breath, transparent background, readable chibi top-down RPG assassin silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Bal-Bal, Philippine grave-scent ghoul, long claws, gray-purple skin, sharp teeth, glowing nostrils, funeral cloth, cemetery fog, eerie but not graphic RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Bal-Bal sniffs the air, grave mist spreads low across the battlefield, it vanishes into fog, then claws rise from below the marked enemy.
monsters.design_notes: Avoid gore. Focus on deception, grave mist, scent tracking, and burial respect.

monsters.province_origin: Western Visayas and wider Visayan oral traditions
monsters.passive_ability: Stiff-Limbed Pursuit — Immune to Fear and resistant to knockback. Takes extra damage from River or cleansing water skills.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Rigid Grave March — Advances with unnatural stiffness, dealing Underworld damage and applying Slow to the target.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Cold Hands of Return — Grabs all nearby enemies with corpse-cold hands, applying Root and reducing speed.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Heavy Strike, Slow, Root Prison, Guard, Fortify, Curse, Fear Cry, Weaken, Stone Skin, Rot, Taunt, Counter Ready, Dark Pulse, Protect Ally, Spirit Leak
monsters.capture_difficulty: Medium-Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires leading the unfallen dead to running water and completing final rites
monsters.habitat: Graveyard, Ancestral House, Riverbank, Corrupted Wilds, Old Chapel Ruins
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Running water, burial cloth, candle, final prayer, clean soil
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Amalanhig are known in Visayan folklore as corpse-like beings or the walking dead, often imagined as stiff bodies that pursue the living. In ALAMAT, an Amalanhig forms when the dead cannot fall properly into rest. Some are created by failed rituals, others by unfinished anger, and others by corruption that locks a body upright after the soul has already tried to leave. This Amalanhig follows villagers at night, arms outstretched, not to kill them but because it cannot remember where its grave ends and the road begins. The player must guide it toward running water, recover its burial cloth, and complete the last words that were never spoken. Once bonded, Amalanhig becomes a slow, durable control tank that roots enemies, reduces speed, and refuses to fall even when heavily damaged.
monsters.folklore_summary: Amalanhig are described in Visayan folklore as undead or corpse-like beings, often connected with stiff movement, pursuit of the living, and vulnerability to water or rivers in some retellings.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT frames Amalanhig as an unfallen dead being: not merely undead, but a body and memory unable to complete the journey into rest.
monsters.physical_description: A stiff walking corpse-spirit wrapped in faded burial cloth, with earth-gray skin, closed or milky eyes, rigid arms, cracked clay-like joints, and cold blue light glowing from the chest.
monsters.weaknesses: River, Sacred cleansing, running water, final rites, Sun purification
monsters.resistances: Underworld, Fear, Root, Slow, Poison, physical knockback
monsters.npc_related: Aling Sabel the midwife; Apo Samad the grave keeper; Tino the ferryman
monsters.boss_variant: Amalanhig ng Saradong Libingan — summons stiff corpse echoes from sealed graves.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Gapos-Amalanhig — bound by corruption chains and spreads Slow every turn.
monsters.divine_variant: Napahingang Bantay — rested dead guardian wrapped in clean white light.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A dry breath through burial cloth, followed by stiff footsteps and a faint unfinished word.
monsters.drop_items: Burial Cloth Strip, Cold Clay Joint, Restless Candle Wax, River-Stone Mark
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Bone Talisman, Woven Garb, Bell, Anting-Anting, Totem
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Amalanhig inspired by Visayan folklore: stiff walking dead spirit, faded burial cloth, earth-gray skin, rigid arms, milky eyes, cracked clay joints, cold blue chest light, transparent background, readable chibi RPG tank silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Amalanhig, Visayan walking dead folklore being, burial cloth, gray clay-like skin, milky eyes, cold blue chest glow, graveyard and river mist background, tragic RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Amalanhig marches forward with rigid steps, cold mist gathers at its hands, the ground cracks, and corpse-cold arms rise to root enemies in place.
monsters.design_notes: Slow tank-control identity. Treat as tragic unrest, not zombie gore.

monsters.province_origin: Ilocos, Pangasinan, and neighboring northern traditions
monsters.passive_ability: Headless Dread — Immune to Blind and resistant to fear. Enemies that attack Pugot for the first time may suffer Fear.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Headless Cleave — Swings a spectral blade in a wide arc, dealing Shadow/Physical damage and lowering enemy attack.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Vanishing Neck-Mist — Dissolves into mist, removes Marked from itself, and reappears behind the strongest enemy with a counter strike.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Heavy Strike, Shadow Step, Guard Break, Fear Cry, Smoke Veil, Counter Ready, Fortify, Quick Slash, Weaken, Dark Pulse, Taunt, Haste, Spirit Ward, Ambush, Silence
monsters.capture_difficulty: Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires returning the lost head or restoring the truth of its death
monsters.habitat: Spanish-Era Road, Graveyard, Old Chapel Ruins, Ancestral House, Foggy Road
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Black cloth, candle, blade oil, name ribbon, grave flowers
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Pugot is a headless night wanderer known in northern Luzon stories, often feared for appearing suddenly on roads, near old places, or in the dark. In ALAMAT, Pugot are spirits separated from truth. Some lost their heads in violence; others lost their names, honor, or testimony. This Pugot appears on an old road where travelers hear footsteps beside them but see no face. It does not speak, because its voice was buried with the missing head. To earn trust, the player must investigate the old death, recover the lost head-symbol from a hidden shrine, and reveal the betrayal that caused the wandering. Once bonded, Pugot becomes a durable warrior who resists fear and blindness, strikes from mist, and punishes enemies who rely on intimidation.
monsters.folklore_summary: Pugot is a headless being in Ilocano and northern Philippine folklore. It is commonly described as a frightening black or headless apparition, sometimes associated with roads, darkness, and sudden encounters.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT turns Pugot into a truth-seeking headless spirit whose missing head represents a stolen identity, silenced testimony, or unresolved death.
monsters.physical_description: A tall headless humanoid wrapped in dark northern cloth, carrying a spectral bolo-like blade, with black mist rising from the neck, glowing chest markings, heavy feet, and a shadow where the head should be.
monsters.weaknesses: Truth rituals, Sacred light, Sun, bell chimes, restored-name offerings
monsters.resistances: Shadow, Underworld, Blind, Fear, silence, mist concealment
monsters.npc_related: Apo Raniag the old witness; Captain Isko the road patrolman; Elena the descendant of the accused
monsters.boss_variant: Pugot ng Lumang Daan — creates multiple headless mist copies along the road.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Pugot na Walang Pangalan — attacks anyone who speaks a false name.
monsters.divine_variant: Bantay ng Katotohanan — restored headless guardian carrying a blade of clear light.
monsters.cry_voice_description: No voice at first—only footsteps, cloth movement, and a hollow exhale from the neck mist.
monsters.drop_items: Neck Mist Cloth, Spectral Blade Chip, Lost Name Ribbon, Roadside Candle
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Relic Weapon, Woven Garb, Bell, Anting-Anting, Spirit Mask
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Pugot inspired by northern Philippine folklore: tall headless night wanderer, dark woven clothing, black mist rising from neck, spectral bolo-like blade, glowing chest markings, transparent background, readable chibi top-down RPG warrior silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Pugot, Ilocano/northern Luzon headless spirit, dark cloth, black neck mist, spectral blade, old foggy road background, tragic and intimidating RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Pugot dissolves into roadside fog, footsteps circle the enemy, a spectral blade flashes from behind, and black neck mist forms a fear glyph before vanishing.
monsters.design_notes: Do not overuse gore. The missing head is symbolic: stolen truth, lost identity, and unresolved death.
